source.title: Dissociable components of rule-guided behavior depend on distinct medial and prefrontal regions

confidence_rationale: Circumscribed lesion study with double dissociation showing principal sulcus (lateral PFC) is selectively necessary for abstract rule maintenance in working memory. Strong causal claim from a high-prestige venue. The localization maps precisely to MECH-262's lateral-PFC-analog. Mapping fidelity to signature 2 (distractor-resistant persistence) is high. Signatures 1 and 3 are not directly tested.
failure_signatures: Lesion to principal sulcus impairs rule maintenance in working memory but also impairs other aspects of the WCST task; the deficit is not cleanly isolated to rule persistence under distraction as MECH-262 signature 2 requires.
Principal sulcus lesions in macaques may compensate over time via other frontal regions, so the acute lesion deficit may overstate the normal role of this region in rule maintenance.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Circumscribed lesions to the principal sulcus (lateral PFC) selectively impaired maintenance of abstract rules in working memory in a WCST analog; OFC lesions impaired rule-value updating; ACC sulcus lesions impaired use of recent choice-outcome values. The triple dissociation confirms that abstract rule maintenance is specifically dependent on lateral PFC.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-262 locates rule-selective persistence specifically in the lateral-PFC-analog (SD-033a). Buckley et al's double/triple dissociation provides causal evidence that the biological correlate of this substrate -- the principal sulcus -- is selectively necessary for abstract rule maintenance. If the lateral-PFC-analog is ablated or its write gate disabled in REE, task-rule maintenance should fail while reward-based updating (OFC-analog) and recent-outcome reference (ACC-analog, MECH-260) should be spared.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The WCST analog tests rule maintenance over seconds-to-minutes, which is longer than the trial-level WM tested in electrophysiology studies, but shorter than the multi-episode persistence MECH-262 requires in V3. The lesion approach cannot distinguish between failure of rule encoding, failure of persistence, or failure of retrieval.
mapping.source_context: Macaque circumscribed lesion study (OFC, principal sulcus, superior dlPFC, vlPFC, ACC sulcus), WCST analog task, n = 5 per group

confidence_components.notes: Science publication, causal lesion design with clear dissociation. Mapping fidelity high for the lateral-PFC localization claim and for signature 2 (rule persistence). Slightly reduced because the behavioral measure (WCST error rate) is coarser than the mechanistic three-signature framework of MECH-262. Transfer risk low: macaque principal sulcus is the canonical referent for the REE lateral-PFC-analog.
